title: Amber Current
tagline: 600 particles drift through a Perlin-noise flow field in warm amber light
technique: canvas / perlin noise
description: 600 particles drift through a Perlin-noise flow field, each following the vector angle defined by 2D noise sampled at its position, leaving ghost-like streaks that reveal the underlying current structure in a palette of deep amber, molten orange, and pale gold.

title: Still Oscillating
tagline: Dual-pendulum harmonograph traces decaying Lissajous curves in violet light
technique: canvas / harmonograph / parametric equations
description: A harmonograph simulation with two coupled pendulums driving a virtual XY pen plotter, tracing Lissajous-family curves that decay as the pendulums lose energy, with thin strokes on a near-black canvas that dissolve and restart with slightly varied frequencies.

title: Between the Tiles
tagline: A binary Truchet grid slowly ripples as random tiles flip orientation forever
technique: canvas / truchet tiles / quarter-circle arcs
description: Each cell in a grid independently picks one of two Truchet quarter-circle arc orientations, creating a labyrinthine network that slowly ripples as random tiles flip orientation every 80 ms, in a woodblock-print contrast of deep ink and warm cream.

title: Every Seed Knows
tagline: ~800 seeds spiral outward via the golden angle; a static botanical SVG with zero JavaScript
technique: SVG / phyllotaxis / golden angle
description: ~800 seeds spiral outward from a center using the golden angle (137.508°), the same geometry a sunflower uses for gap-free packing, with dot radius scaling from 1.5 px at center to 5 px at edge in a botanical palette of sage, ochre, terracotta, cream, and charcoal.

title: The Room Divides Itself
tagline: A square canvas recursively subdivided by a seeded random process — architectural yet organic
technique: SVG / recursive quadtree subdivision
description: A square canvas subdivides recursively by a seeded random process with decreasing probability at each depth, preferring to split along the longer axis and avoiding slivers, with ~22% of leaf rectangles resting in warm white to provide breathing room among earthy colors.

title: The Surface Speaks
tagline: A rippling sine–cosine surface sampled onto an 80×40 ASCII grid — amber phosphor on deep navy
technique: HTML pre / ASCII art / sine-cosine product surface
description: An 80×40 character grid samples a mathematically infinite 3D sine-cosine product surface, with z values mapped to a nine-character brightness ramp, rendered as amber phosphor text on deep navy in real-time as the diagonal-traveling wave crest animates at 60 fps.

title: Light Through Cells
tagline: 25 drifting Voronoi seeds painted in warm amber and teal — a stained-glass window that never settles
technique: canvas / Voronoi diagram / brute-force pixel coloring
description: A Voronoi diagram computed via brute-force pixel coloring with 25 drifting seed points, whose cells shift continuously to create the impression of light filtering through a stained-glass window with lead-came borders and colors assigned round-robin to contrast adjacent cells.

title: Where Life Begins
tagline: Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion grows coral Turing patterns from a single seed — charcoal becomes cream
technique: canvas / reaction-diffusion / Gray-Scott
description: Reaction-diffusion at Gray-Scott parameters F=0.0545 and K=0.062 grows branching coral-like structures from a single seeded patch, with U concentration feeding an autocatalytic reaction that produces V while diffusion spreads both species across a toroidal grid.

title: Frequencies in Common
tagline: 56 Lissajous curves — 14 frequency ratios × 4 phase offsets — woven in near-black navy on warm cream
technique: SVG / Lissajous curves / parametric / pen-plotter
description: 56 Lissajous curves — 14 frequency ratios from 1:2 through 5:7 with four phase offsets each — overlap and weave across an 800×800 canvas in thin strokes at 25% opacity, with overlapping regions building density gradients the way a physical plotter would.

title: Dot by Dot the Light Falls
tagline: A 60×60 halftone grid maps a damped radial ripple to dot radii — fluorescent coral on cream, slowly expanding
technique: canvas / halftone grid / damped radial ripple / risograph
description: A 60×60 halftone grid where each cell's dot radius is determined by a damped radial ripple function that expands outward over 8 seconds, with fluorescent coral ink on cream paper and a deep navy misregistration shadow offset by two pixels to replicate risograph printing.

title: The Plant That Writes Itself
tagline: A fractal fern grown by a context-free rewriting rule — 6,048 segments appear one by one over 4 seconds, then the plant fades and regrows
technique: canvas / L-system / turtle graphics
description: An L-system fractal plant expands from axiom X through 6 iterations into 6,048 F-segments, then a turtle-graphics interpreter draws it branch by branch over 4 seconds, holds for 1 second, fades to nothing in 0.5 seconds, and regrows in a seamless loop.

title: The Shape of Distance
tagline: A WebGL ray-marcher sphere-traces signed-distance fields — torus and sphere blend in warm gold, casting soft shadows and reflected light, driven by pure math
technique: WebGL / SDF ray marching / GLSL fragment shader
description: Ray marching through a signed-distance field blends a rotating torus with a morphing sphere via smooth union, adds a dark-slate ground plane and chrome reflective sphere, and computes ambient occlusion and soft shadows against a warm point light to render warm gold surfaces emerging from deep indigo fog.

title: Letters in Motion
tagline: The word DRIFT rasterised into ~3 000 particles that assemble, hold, then scatter and fade in a seamless loop — generative typography via canvas pixel-sampling
technique: canvas / generative typography / particle system
description: The word DRIFT is rasterized from a hidden canvas into ~3,000 particles that begin at random screen positions, lerp toward their target coordinates with diminishing sinusoidal wobble, hold gently at rest, then scatter outward with accelerating velocity before fading to nothing.

title: Two Truths Overlapping
tagline: Two families of concentric rings evaluated per-pixel beat against each other — a moiré interference field that breathes and rotates in a seamless 25-second cycle
technique: canvas / ImageData / moiré interference / per-pixel sinusoidal evaluation
description: Two families of concentric rings interfere per-pixel, with centers on different 25-second and 27-second cycles, producing hyperbolic interference fringes that rotate and breathe as moiré patterns with wavelength oscillating between 20 and 35 px.

title: No Pattern Repeats
tagline: 2,330 thick and thin rhombi grown from a ten-triangle sun by six generations of golden-ratio deflation — an aperiodic Penrose P3 tiling in deep indigo and warm amber
technique: SVG / Penrose P3 tiling / substitution deflation
description: A Penrose P3 tiling of thick and thin rhombi generated by six generations of golden-ratio deflation from a ten-triangle sun, producing 2,330 triangles that tile aperiodically with five-fold rotational symmetry in deep indigo and warm amber on a near-black background.

title: Circles All the Way Down
tagline: 376 circles packed by Descartes' theorem fill every interstice of an Apollonian gasket — deep teal outermost, warming through gold and coral to cream at the finest scale
technique: SVG / Apollonian gasket / Descartes' Circle Theorem / Apollonian reflection
description: 376 circles packed by Descartes' Circle Theorem fill every interstice of an Apollonian gasket, starting from three equal circles inside an enclosing circle and recursively filling each triangular gap with its unique inscribed circle, with colors warming from deep teal outermost to cream at finest scale.

title: Dust of the Infinite
tagline: 2 million iterations of the Clifford strange attractor accumulate as fractal dust — amber filaments and rose peaks emerge from deterministic chaos on a near-black canvas
technique: canvas / Clifford attractor / density accumulation / chunked setTimeout
description: 2 million iterations of the Clifford strange attractor are binned into an 800×800 accumulation buffer with logarithmic density mapping and gamma compression, revealing amber filaments and rose peaks that accumulate into a glowing fractal dust on a near-black canvas.

title: Circles Within Circles
tagline: 50 rotating epicycles, each spinning at an integer multiple of the base frequency, chain together to trace a glowing heart — Fourier decomposition made visible
technique: canvas / Fourier epicycles / DFT / requestAnimationFrame
description: A heart shape traced by 50 rotating epicycles chained tip-to-tail, each spinning at an integer multiple of the base frequency, reconstructs the original signal through Fourier decomposition where the largest epicycle sweeps out the overall round shape and smaller circles refine the lobes and cusp.

title: Superformula Bloom
tagline: The Gielis superformula morphs through six natural archetypes — flower, star, snowflake, leaf — in a seamless 30-second botanical loop
technique: canvas / Gielis superformula / parametric morphing / requestAnimationFrame
description: The Gielis superformula morphs through six natural archetypes — flower, star, snowflake, leaf — in a seamless 30-second cycle by slowly varying m, n1, n2, and n3 parameters, with three concentric depth layers and a translucent rose fill wash creating the look of a layered botanical illustration.

title: Every Point, Once
tagline: A Hilbert curve of order 6 threads 4,095 segments through a 64×64 grid — drawn stroke by stroke in copper on near-black, visiting every cell exactly once
technique: canvas / Hilbert curve / space-filling / recursive subdivision
description: A Hilbert curve of order 6 threads 4,095 segments through a 64×64 grid, drawn stroke by stroke in copper on near-black, visiting every cell exactly once in a non-self-intersecting path that appears over 50 seconds before holding and restarting.

title: Closed and Infinite
tagline: Six hypotrochoid presets draw themselves stroke by stroke — petals of 5, 7, 8, 9, 11, and 13 unfold one by one, each in a distinct palette, fading seamlessly into the next
technique: canvas / hypotrochoid / roulette curve / parametric
description: Six hypotrochoid presets — curves where a pen attached to a small rolling circle traces as it rolls inside a larger fixed circle — are drawn stroke by stroke in distinct palettes, each completing in 22 seconds before a 1.2-second fade transitions to the next preset.

title: Julia Set — Orbit-Trap Coloring
tagline: Each pixel's orbit under z→z²+c is trap-coloured from deep indigo to amber to ivory — a jewel-like fractal that morphs in real time as c drifts along a slow closed path
technique: WebGL / Julia set / orbit-trap coloring / GLSL fragment shader
description: For each pixel the orbit under z→z²+c is computed and the minimum distance to the origin is recorded as an orbit-trap value, driving a three-stop palette from deep indigo through amber to ivory, while c drifts along a slow closed circle to keep the Julia set connected.

title: The Pond Remembers Every Stone
tagline: Five coherent wave sources drift on Lissajous orbits — their superposed wavefronts paint shifting indigo and amber interference fringes across a full-bleed canvas, pixel by pixel, at 60 fps
technique: canvas / wave superposition / interference / ImageData per-pixel
description: Five point wave sources emit coherent circular wavefronts as they drift on Lissajous orbits, with superposed amplitudes mapped through a 256-step palette — deep indigo/violet for negative, near-black at zero crossing, warm gold/amber for positive — forming shifting hyperbolic interference fringes at 60 fps.

title: Brian's Brain: The Spark That Cannot Rest
tagline: Three-state cellular automata — OFF, ON, DYING — generate glider swarms racing across a toroidal grid; electric teal sparks and violet trails on a near-black canvas at 25 fps
technique: canvas / cellular automata / Brian's Brain / Uint8Array double-buffer
description: Three-state cellular automata with OFF, ON, and DYING states generate glider swarms on a toroidal grid where an OFF cell ignites if exactly two of its eight neighbors are ON, an ON cell immediately dies, and a DYING cell returns to OFF, producing self-propagating patterns from random seeding.

title: Chladni Figures — Standing Waves Made Visible
tagline: Six standing-wave eigenmodes evaluated per pixel reveal glowing nodal lines on deep indigo — the sand patterns of a vibrating plate rendered in pure mathematics at 60 fps
technique: canvas / Chladni eigenmodes / ImageData per-pixel / crossfade animation
description: Six standing-wave eigenmodes evaluated per-pixel reveal glowing nodal lines on deep indigo, where the antisymmetric eigenmode function traces the still points a vibrating plate would have at those frequencies, cycling with smooth crossfades every 1.5 seconds.

title: String Art — The Curve Lives Between the Lines
tagline: 300 chords connect numbered points on a circle; as the real-valued multiplier eases from 2 to 50 the envelope morphs through cardioid, nephroid, and stranger epicycloid families — dusty rose on near-black at 60 fps
technique: canvas / string art / epicycloid envelopes / fractional modulo interpolation
description: 300 chords connect numbered points on a circle, with the multiplier oscillating from 2 to 50 so the envelope morphs through cardioid, nephroid, and stranger epicycloid families, with overlapping lines at 35% opacity building luminosity at the densest parts of the invisible curve they trace.

title: Rhodonea — The Petal Count Is Always a Surprise
tagline: Three concentric rose curves r = cos(k·θ) morph continuously as k sweeps 1→6, doubling or halving petal count at each rational — gold on deep charcoal with motion-blur ghosting at 30 fps
technique: canvas / rhodonea polar curves / rational k morphing / motion-blur ghosting
description: Three concentric rose curves r = cos(k·θ) morph continuously as k sweeps 1 to 6, doubling or halving petal count at each rational, with motion-blur ghosting letting recent curves glow while older strokes decay at 30 fps.

title: The Land Before Memory — Contours of a Dreamed Terrain
tagline: A 3-D value-noise heightfield sampled at 120×90 evolves slowly over time; marching squares extracts 12 isolines in a geological palette — deep indigo sea floor to cream snowcap — with thicker index contours every 5th level at 60 fps
technique: canvas / marching squares / 3-D value noise / fBm / topographic contours
description: A 3-D value-noise heightfield sampled at 120×90 evolves slowly over time, with marching squares extracting 12 isolines in a geological palette from deep indigo sea floor to cream snowcap, with thicker index contours every 5th level following cartographic convention at 60 fps.

title: Every Corner Has a Choice
tagline: A 30×30 grid of Smith-variant Truchet tiles — each cell a binary arc choice — slowly reshuffles every 4 seconds, tracing rivers and closed loops from memoryless randomness; ivory ground, deep indigo arcs
technique: canvas / Truchet tiles / Smith quarter-circle arc variant / crossfade animation
description: A 30×30 grid of Smith-variant Truchet tiles — each cell choosing one of two quarter-circle arc orientations — slowly reshuffles every 4 seconds, tracing rivers and closed loops from memoryless randomness in ivory ground with deep indigo arcs.

title: The Signal Disagrees With Itself
tagline: Concentric rectangles in six saturated colors pulse through three glitch techniques — scanline displacement, RGB channel split, and block copy — building from calm geometry to chromatic chaos on a 7-second cycle
technique: canvas / ImageData / glitch art / scanline displacement / chromatic aberration / block copy
description: Concentric rectangles in six saturated colors pulse through three glitch techniques over a 7-second cycle — scanline displacement, chromatic aberration, and block copy — building from calm geometry to chromatic chaos in a canvas ImageData simulation.

title: The Fern That Was Always Equations
tagline: Barnsley fern rendered via the chaos game — four weighted affine transforms accumulate 2 million random points into an emergent botanical fractal; emerald, lime, gold, and pearl palette morphs between fern, tree, and spiral presets on a 12-second cycle
technique: canvas / IFS chaos game / affine transforms / log-density rendering / morphing animation
description: A Barnsley fern is rendered via the chaos game where 2 million random points are weighted by four affine transforms, accumulating into an emergent botanical fractal in emerald, lime, gold, and pearl that morphs between fern, tree, and spiral presets on a 12-second cycle.

title: DLA Crystal — Frost on Glass
tagline: 40 000 particles random-walk from the canvas edge until they touch the aggregate and freeze; birth order maps to a deep-blue→copper gradient, revealing the full growth history of a dendritic DLA fractal in a seamless 30-second loop
technique: canvas / diffusion-limited aggregation / ImageData / Uint8Array occupancy grid / requestAnimationFrame
description: 40,000 particles random-walk from the canvas edge until they touch the aggregate and freeze, with birth order mapped to a deep-blue to copper gradient, revealing the full growth history of a dendritic diffusion-limited aggregation fractal in a 30-second seamless loop.

title: Wolfram Elementary CA — Rule 30 / 90 / 110
tagline: Three Wolfram elementary rules — chaotic Rule 30, Sierpiński-fractal Rule 90, Turing-complete Rule 110 — each grow from a single center cell and scroll downward in amber, teal, and rose on near-black, cycling with smooth cross-fades every nine seconds
technique: canvas / cellular automata / Wolfram elementary rules / ImageData / Uint8Array scrolling / requestAnimationFrame
description: Three Wolfram elementary rules — chaotic Rule 30, Sierpiński-fractal Rule 90, and Turing-complete Rule 110 — each grow from a single center cell downward on a scrolling canvas in amber, teal, and rose, cycling with smooth cross-fades every 9 seconds.

title: Harmonograph — The Pendulum Writes Itself
tagline: Five damped-pendulum presets trace decaying Lissajous figures stroke by stroke — 100 000 points accumulate over eight seconds, line width tapering from 2.5 px to hairline as the amplitude fades, in warm cream, chalk, amber, gold, and white on near-black
technique: canvas / harmonograph / damped pendulum / Lissajous / requestAnimationFrame
description: Five damped-pendulum presets trace decaying Lissajous figures stroke by stroke with 100,000 points accumulating over 8 seconds, line width tapering from 2.5 px to hairline as amplitude fades in warm cream, chalk, amber, gold, and white on near-black.

title: Newton's Basin — Fractal Roots
tagline: Four polynomials (z³−1, z⁴−1, z⁵−1, z³−2z+2) iterated via Newton's method in a WebGL fragment shader — each pixel colours to the root it converges to, brightness falling toward fractal boundaries, cycling every 8 seconds through triskelion, checkerboard, flower, and asymmetric swirl
technique: WebGL / Newton fractal / complex iteration / GLSL
description: Four polynomials (z³−1, z⁴−1, z⁵−1, z³−2z+2) are iterated via Newton's method in a WebGL fragment shader, with each pixel colored by the root it converges to, brightness falling toward fractal boundaries, cycling every 8 seconds through triskelion, checkerboard, flower, and asymmetric swirl.

title: Vascular Branching — Space Colonization
tagline: 500 attractor points distributed in an ellipse are consumed one by one as a skeleton of branch nodes competes for their direction — the Runions algorithm grows coral-like vasculature from a single root, tapering from 4 px trunk to hairline tips, in terracotta on cream
technique: canvas / space colonization / Runions algorithm / incremental stroke rendering
description: 500 attractor points distributed in an ellipse are consumed one by one as branch nodes compete for their direction via the Runions space-colonization algorithm, growing coral-like vasculature from a single root tapered from 4 px trunk to hairline tips in terracotta on cream.

title: Belousov-Zhabotinsky — Oscillating Spiral Waves
tagline: A 300 × 300 Greenberg-Hastings cellular automaton on a toroidal grid — quiescent, excited, and 4-step refractory states self-organise into counter-rotating spiral waves, rendered cell-by-cell into an ImageData buffer in electric teal fading to coral on deep navy
technique: canvas / cellular automaton / Greenberg-Hastings / ImageData / requestAnimationFrame
description: A 300×300 Greenberg-Hastings cellular automaton on a toroidal grid simulates the Belousov-Zhabotinsky chemical oscillator with quiescent, excited, and four-step refractory states, self-organizing into counter-rotating spiral waves in electric teal fading to coral on deep navy.

title: Phyllotaxis — The Angle the Sunflower Chose
tagline: 1 500 seeds grow outward one per frame at the golden angle (137.508°), then the angle morphs ±10° revealing visible spokes before recovering the dense irrational packing — saffron-to-rust palette on near-black
technique: canvas / phyllotaxis / golden angle / Fermat spiral / morphing animation / requestAnimationFrame
description: 1,500 seeds grow outward one per frame at the golden angle (137.508°), then the angle morphs ±10° revealing visible spokes before recovering the dense irrational packing, demonstrating why the golden angle prevents clustering in a saffron-to-rust palette on near-black.

title: The Grain That Broke the Symmetry
tagline: 65 536 grains dropped at the center of a 401×401 grid topple outward until stable — self-organized criticality draws a fractal with exact 4-fold symmetry from a single rule, rendered cell-by-cell via ImageData
technique: canvas / abelian sandpile / self-organized criticality / ImageData
description: 65,536 grains dropped at the center of a 401×401 grid topple outward until stable, with any cell holding four or more grains shedding one grain to each cardinal neighbor, producing a fractal with exact 4-fold symmetry from self-organized criticality rendered via ImageData.

title: Rotating Polyhedra — Wireframe in Space
tagline: Three nested Platonic solids — tetrahedron, icosahedron, dodecahedron — spin on different axes at different speeds; perspective projection and depth-cued opacity turn pure geometry into a pen-plotter poem
technique: canvas / 3D perspective projection / rotation matrices / wireframe
description: Three nested Platonic solids — tetrahedron, icosahedron, and dodecahedron — spin on different axes at different speeds, rendered as cream wireframes on deep-charcoal via perspective projection with depth-cued opacity giving maximum transparency to far edges and full opacity to near edges.

title: Penrose Tiling — Fivefold Symmetry
tagline: 2 120 thick and thin rhombi grown from a ten-triangle sun by six generations of golden-ratio deflation — an aperiodic P3 Penrose tiling in terracotta and dusty sage on parchment, evoking Islamic geometric tilework
technique: canvas / Penrose P3 tiling / substitution deflation / golden ratio
description: 2,120 thick and thin rhombi grown from a ten-triangle sun by six generations of golden-ratio deflation create an aperiodic P3 Penrose tiling with perfect local fivefold symmetry that never repeats globally, in terracotta and dusty sage on parchment evoking Islamic geometric tilework.

title: Dragon Curve — The Fold That Never Repeats
tagline: 32 768 segments of an order-15 dragon curve grow one by one from a single point — each segment coloured teal through rose by the bit-sequence paper-folding algorithm, filling an 800 × 800 canvas in a seamless 27-second loop
technique: canvas / dragon curve / paper-folding / bit-sequence algorithm / requestAnimationFrame
description: 32,768 segments of an order-15 dragon curve grow one by one from a single point, each colored teal through rose by the bit-sequence paper-folding algorithm that computes turns in O(1) time without storing the full sequence, filling an 800×800 canvas in a 27-second loop.

title: Mitosis in Amber
tagline: Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion at F=0.0545, k=0.062 grows coral Turing patterns from a seeded center — near-black ground blooms into warm amber filaments and ivory peaks at 60 fps on a 512×512 canvas
technique: canvas / reaction-diffusion / Gray-Scott / Float32Array double-buffer / ImageData
description: Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion at F=0.0545 and K=0.062 grows coral Turing patterns from a seeded center on a 512×512 canvas, mapping V concentration from near-black ground through warm amber filaments to ivory peaks at 60 fps.

title: SDF Metaballs — Blobscape
tagline: Eight metaballs drift on irrational-frequency Lissajous paths, merging and separating through a per-pixel SDF field — deep violet cores bleed to cyan edges with a specular highlight at every iso-surface boundary
technique: canvas / signed-distance field / metaballs / ImageData / finite-difference gradient
description: Eight metaballs drift on quasi-periodic Lissajous paths with irrational frequency ratios ensuring paths never close, rendering pixel-by-pixel with a 2D potential field where blobs merge smoothly, colored from deep violet cores through cyan edges to specular highlights.

title: Hyperbolic Tiling — Poincaré Disk
tagline: A {5,4} hyperbolic tessellation of pentagons fills the Poincaré disk, slowly rotating via Möbius transform — amber tiles on deep indigo converge to the boundary in Escher-like recursive symmetry
technique: canvas / hyperbolic geometry / Poincaré disk / Möbius transforms / BFS tile generation
description: A {5,4} hyperbolic tessellation of pentagons fills the Poincaré disk via BFS tile generation, slowly rotating through Möbius transforms, revealing Escher-like recursive symmetry with amber tiles on deep indigo converging toward the boundary circle.

title: Highway Builders — Langton's Ant and Turmite Variants
tagline: Five Langton's-Ant agents race across a 512×512 toroidal grid — classic RL builds its diagonal highway while RLR, LLRR, RLLR, and LRRL spin spirals and rings in amber, cyan, green, violet, and rose
technique: canvas / Langton's Ant / turmite / Uint8Array grid / ImageData per-pixel / requestAnimationFrame
description: Five Langton's Ant agents share a 512×512 toroidal grid, each driven by a different rule string that governs turning at current cell colors, building characteristic highways, spirals, and rings in amber, cyan, green, violet, and rose as 200 steps batch per frame.

title: Bifurcation: Where Order Fractures Into Chaos
tagline: The logistic map x → r·x·(1−x) sweeps from stable fixed point through period-doubling cascades to full chaos — a per-pixel canvas render that builds left to right, shifting from cool blue to warm gold as r crosses the Feigenbaum onset at 3.57
technique: canvas / logistic map / bifurcation diagram / ImageData per-pixel / requestAnimationFrame
description: The logistic map x→r·x·(1−x) sweeps from stable fixed point through period-doubling cascades to full chaos as r advances from 2.5 to 4.0, with each r-column built from 300 iterates plotting the long-run attractor, colored from cool blue (order) to warm gold (chaos).

title: Murmuration: Three Rules and a Sky
tagline: 300 boids follow separation, alignment, and cohesion — three local rules produce the fluid murmurations of starlings on a deep midnight-blue canvas at 60 fps
technique: Boids / Particle System
description: 300 boids follow three local rules — separation, alignment, and cohesion — within a 60 px perception radius, producing fluid murmurations that split, wheel, and re-cohere without central coordination on a deep midnight-blue canvas.

title: The Field Remembers
tagline: 4 000 particles follow a slowly-evolving simplex-noise vector field, leaving near-invisible ink trails in navy, rust, and sage on cream — building dense layered compositions before the canvas resets every 600 frames
technique: canvas / flow field / Perlin noise / particle trails
description: 4,000 particles drift through a smoothly-varying 2D simplex-noise vector field that evolves over time, leaving faint ink trails in navy, rust, and sage that accumulate into dense layered compositions before the canvas clears every 600 frames for a new palimpsest.

title: Celtic Knotwork: Over, Under, Forever
tagline: A (3,5) torus knot projected to 2D — one bronze strand weaves over and under itself ten times on deep forest green, rendered with correct over-under crossings and a subtle oscillating tilt
technique: canvas / torus knot / parametric curve / over-under crossing / painter's algorithm
description: A (3,5) torus knot projects to 2D and renders as one strand weaving over and under itself ten times in warm bronze on deep forest green, with crossing depth determined by tilting the 3D curve and erasing then repainting strands to create convincing over-under illusions.

title: Ink Dropped in Still Water: Paper Marbling
tagline: Five Ebru stone drops build a concentric-ring colour field in terracotta, cobalt, saffron, and green on warm cream; two comb passes sweep it into swirling marbled bands — animated build-up on an 800×800 ImageData canvas
technique: canvas / paper marbling / Ebru / ImageData per-pixel / inverse warp / Gaussian comb drag
description: Turkish Ebru marbling is simulated with five concentric-ring color field stone drops followed by two comb passes that sweep it into swirling bands, using inverse-warp deformations that push ink radially outward and displace pixels along comb tines with Gaussian falloff.

title: Torus Knot Curves
tagline: A (p,q) torus knot traced stroke-by-stroke on a dark canvas — line weight varies sinusoidally by depth to fake near/far occlusion; four classic knots cycle on demand: trefoil, cinquefoil, star, and septafoil
technique: parametric-3d-curves
description: A (p,q) torus knot is traced stroke-by-stroke with line width proportional to depth for fake near-far occlusion, cycling through four classic knots — trefoil, cinquefoil, star, and septafoil — on a dark background with warm accent colors assigned by depth band.

title: Strange Attractor: A Basin with No Bottom
tagline: A Clifford strange attractor iterated for 5 million steps — additive per-pixel accumulation on deep-violet builds the butterfly shape through density, coloured from violet through sky-blue to gold
technique: canvas / clifford attractor / ImageData additive blending / strange attractor
description: A Clifford strange attractor iterated for 5 million steps accumulates through additive per-pixel blending on deep-violet, with point color derived from y-coordinate mapping through violet to sky-blue to gold, revealing the butterfly shape through density structure without exact repetition.

title: Voronoi Mosaic: Scattered Light
tagline: 200 seed points partition an 800×800 canvas into a stained-glass Voronoi mosaic — 12 jewel tones (sapphire, ruby, amber, jade, amethyst…) with near-black lead-came borders; each seed drifts on a sinusoidal path for slow tectonic-plate morphing at 20 fps
technique: canvas / Voronoi diagram / distance-field scanline / spatial grid / ImageData
description: 200 seed points partition an 800×800 canvas into a Voronoi mosaic colored in 12 jewel tones (sapphire, ruby, amber, jade, amethyst, and more) with near-black lead-came borders, slowly morphing as seeds drift on sinusoidal paths for slow tectonic-plate-like movement.

title: Ghost Frequencies: A Moiré Study
tagline: Two systems of concentric rings overlap on an 800×800 canvas — a fixed system and a slowly drifting one; canvas difference compositing cancels coincident rings to black, producing a shifting moiré interference fringe on a near-black monochrome ground
technique: moiré / optical interference / canvas difference compositing / concentric rings
description: Two systems of concentric rings overlap on an 800×800 canvas — one fixed, one drifting on slow sinusoidal orbit — with canvas difference compositing canceling coincident rings to black and producing shifting moiré interference fringes on near-black monochrome ground.

title: SDF Raymarcher — Infinite Folded Space
tagline: An infinite field of tori rendered entirely in a GLSL fragment shader via sphere-tracing — warm gold surfaces emerge from deep indigo fog as the camera slowly orbits, repeating forever through domain folding
technique: WebGL / SDF ray marching / domain repetition / GLSL fragment shader
description: An infinite field of tori rendered entirely in a GLSL fragment shader via sphere-tracing, with domain repetition folding infinite space into a single cell and warm gold surfaces emerging from deep indigo fog as a slowly orbiting camera reveals the structure from every azimuth.

title: Fourier Epicycles — Drawing with Circles
tagline: A 5-pointed star decomposed into 64 rotating circles via the DFT — cool blue epicycles on deep navy trace warm gold geometry, closing perfectly after each 8-second revolution
technique: canvas / Fourier epicycles / DFT / CSS opacity transition / requestAnimationFrame
description: A 5-pointed star decomposed into 64 rotating circles via the DFT, with each epicycle radius and phase determined by frequency components chained tip-to-tail in descending-amplitude order, so the outermost tip traces the star path, closing perfectly after each 8-second revolution.

title: Lorenz Attractor — The Butterfly That Never Lands
tagline: The 3D Lorenz strange attractor integrated via RK4 — 200 000 trajectory points accumulate into a log-density image on deep indigo as a rotating camera reveals the butterfly from every azimuth in cyan and gold
technique: canvas / Lorenz attractor / RK4 integration / ImageData log-density / rotating camera
description: The 3D Lorenz strange attractor integrated via RK4 at dt=0.005 with 200,000 trajectory points accumulating into a log-density image on deep indigo, mapped to cyan and gold, as a rotating camera reveals the butterfly from every azimuth over a 50-second full revolution.

title: Harmonograph — Four Pendulums, One Stroke
tagline: A bilateral lateral harmonograph with two damped pendulums on each axis — frequency ratios 2:3 and 3:2 with phase offsets trace a near-closed compound Lissajous in cool steel-blue as the curve spirals inward to silence
technique: canvas / bilateral harmonograph / damped pendulum equations / parametric animation
description: A bilateral lateral harmonograph with two damped pendulums on each axis, inverted frequency ratios (2:3 on x, 3:2 on y), and phase offsets producing a near-closed compound Lissajous that traces a complex six-fold symmetric web in cool steel-blue as damping spirals it inward to silence.

title: Circle Packing — Every Gap Has a Name
tagline: A greedy largest-empty-circle algorithm fills an 800×800 canvas iteratively — largest circles appear first in warm cream, medium circles in amber, and tiny gap-filling circles in deep teal, building a dense organic mosaic at 20 circles per frame
technique: canvas / circle packing / greedy largest-empty-circle / requestAnimationFrame
description: A greedy largest-empty-circle algorithm iteratively fills an 800×800 canvas by sampling 200 random candidates and placing the one with the largest clearance radius, colored by log-radius through deep teal to amber to cream, building an organic mosaic at 20 circles per frame.

title: Light Divided
tagline: ~400 random seeds triangulated by Bowyer-Watson into a stained-glass mosaic — rose to amber to slate, each triangle a facet of quiet geometric light, with a 0.5 px lead-came stroke
technique: SVG / Delaunay triangulation / Bowyer-Watson / generative geometry
description: ~400 random seeds triangulated by Bowyer-Watson into a Delaunay mosaic, each triangle colored rose through amber to slate by its centroid's diagonal position, with a 0.5 px near-black stroke providing a stained-glass lead-came effect.

title: Frequencies in Resonance: Lissajous Web
tagline: A single Lissajous curve drifts through every frequency ratio as b sweeps 0.98→5.02 — low-alpha accumulation builds luminous gem-like webs, hue shifting with each near-integer resonance on a near-black canvas at 60 fps
technique: canvas / Lissajous curves / parametric equations / additive blending / requestAnimationFrame
description: A single Lissajous curve x=sin(3τ), y=sin(bτ) is traced continuously as b drifts upward from 0.98 to 5.02 through every near-integer ratio, with low-alpha accumulation building luminous gem-like webs at resonances, hue shifting with each frequency.

title: Halftone Riso Print: Two-Color Screen
tagline: Two halftone dot screens — fluorescent pink at 45° and teal at 75° — overlap with multiply blending on cream paper; slow misregistration drift animates the offset between screens, reproducing the warmth of physical Riso printing
technique: canvas / halftone / risograph simulation / multiply blend / requestAnimationFrame
description: Two halftone dot screens — fluorescent pink at 45° and teal at 75° — overlap with multiply blending on cream paper, slowly drifting offset to show Riso printing's characteristic misregistration, with radii driven by concentric sine rings producing visible density bands.

title: 2D Wave Equation: Interference Ripples
tagline: Three point sources pulse on a 512×512 grid governed by the 2D wave equation — leapfrog finite-differences propagate circular wavefronts that interfere and reflect off Dirichlet boundaries, mapping displacement to deep navy and warm gold at 60 fps
technique: canvas / 2D wave equation / leapfrog finite-difference / ImageData per-pixel / requestAnimationFrame
description: Three point sources pulse on a 512×512 grid governed by the 2D wave equation with leapfrog finite-differences, generating circular wavefronts that interfere and reflect off Dirichlet boundaries, mapping displacement to deep navy and warm gold at 60 fps.

title: Wet Pigment — Watercolor Wash
tagline: Recursive midpoint subdivision distorts base polygons into organic blobs; 25–40 layers of 5–10 % opacity accumulate into soft watercolor washes across three hue families — warm reds, cool blues, and earthy greens — resetting every 45 s with a slow cream fade
technique: canvas / watercolor simulation / recursive midpoint subdivision / translucent polygon layering / requestAnimationFrame
description: Recursive midpoint subdivision distorts base polygons into organic blobs that are filled 25-40 times at 5-10% opacity, layering translucent polygon fills in three hue families (warm reds, cool blues, earthy greens) to simulate wet-on-wet watercolor where pigment accumulates at drying edges.

title: Apollonian Gasket: Every Gap Holds a Circle
tagline: Three seed circles packed inside an enclosing circle generate a deterministic fractal — Descartes’ Circle Theorem fills every triangular interstice with its unique inscribed circle, revealed level by level from warm amber at depth 0 to cool violet-blue at depth 6
technique: canvas / Apollonian gasket / Descartes’ Circle Theorem / Apollonian reflection formula / BFS / requestAnimationFrame
description: Three equal circles packed inside an enclosing circle seed a deterministic fractal where every triangular interstice is filled by the unique circle tangent to its three bounding circles, using Descartes' Circle Theorem, with colors warming from amber at depth 0 to cool blue at depth 6.

title: Density in Characters: ASCII Scalar Field
tagline: Four superimposed plane waves form a scalar interference field rendered as a full-viewport grid of monospaced characters — field density selects from void through punctuation to ‘@’, hue shifts from deep cyan to warm cream, and animated phases cause the character grid to ripple endlessly at 60 fps
technique: canvas / ASCII art / plane wave superposition / scalar field / fillText / requestAnimationFrame
description: Four superimposed plane waves form a scalar interference field rendered as a full-viewport grid of monospaced characters, where field density selects from void through punctuation to "@", hue shifts from deep cyan to warm cream, and animated phases cause the character grid to ripple.

title: Everything Divides Evenly in the End
tagline: A canvas recursively splits itself by golden-ratio and rule-of-thirds cuts until every rectangle rests in white or a primary De Stijl color — red, blue, or yellow — then quietly dissolves and starts over every 8–12 seconds
technique: canvas / recursive Mondrian subdivision / De Stijl palette / golden-ratio splits / dissolve transition
description: A canvas recursively splits itself by golden-ratio and rule-of-thirds proportions until every rectangle rests below a minimum size or depth limit, filled mostly in white with two or three largest cells receiving Mondrian primary colors, regenerating every 8-12 seconds with a slow cream fade.

title: The Grid That Knows Where It Lives — Conformal Map
tagline: A rectangular coordinate grid breathes through three conformal maps — Joukowski (z + λ/z), square (z²), and Möbius ((z−1)/(z+1)) — each morphing smoothly from identity to full distortion over eight seconds while angles are preserved at every crossing; rose horizontal lines and sky-blue vertical lines overlap with additive compositing so intersections bloom
technique: canvas / conformal mapping / complex analysis / Joukowski transform / parametric grid morphing
description: A rectangular coordinate grid breathes through three conformal maps — Joukowski (z+λ/z), square (z²), and Möbius ((z−1)/(z+1)) — each morphing from identity to full distortion over 8 seconds while angles are preserved at every crossing, with rose and sky-blue lines blending additively.

title: The Mold That Finds the Train Schedule: Physarum
tagline: 12 000 agents deposit chemical trail onto an 800×500 grid; trail diffuses and decays each frame while agents steer toward the strongest signal — three local rules crystallise a vein network in deep violet, electric teal, and warm white at 60 fps
technique: canvas / Physarum polycephalum / slime mold simulation / trail diffusion / chemotaxis / Float32Array / ImageData / requestAnimationFrame
description: 12,000 agents deposit chemical trail that diffuses and decays each frame while agents steer toward the strongest signal, self-organizing vein networks from three local rules mimicking how slime mold solves network optimization, rendered in deep violet, electric teal, and warm white at 60 fps.

title: Radiolaria & Diatom Morphology
tagline: Seven microscopic siliceous organisms — each grown from r = a + b·sin(n·θ) + c·sin(m·θ) — rotate slowly on deep navy; bone-white and pale-gold line art with concentric shells, diamond-tipped spines, and inner lattice detail
technique: SVG / polar equations / sinusoidal radial symmetry / SMIL animation
description: Seven microscopic siliceous organisms grown from r = a + b·sin(n·θ) + c·sin(m·θ), each featuring outer outline, concentric shells, radial spines with diamond tips, and inner lattice detail, rotating slowly on deep navy in bone-white and pale-gold SVG line art.

title: Chemical Dreams — Gray-Scott Mitosis
tagline: Two chemical species on a 512×512 toroidal grid self-organise via reaction-diffusion into a continuously self-replicating population of cyan spots on deep indigo — each spot grows to a critical radius and splits in two at 60 fps
technique: canvas / Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion / mitosis regime / Float32Array / ImageData LUT / requestAnimationFrame
description: Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion in the mitosis regime (F=0.0367, K=0.0649) grows circular spots that elongate and split into daughter spots on a 512×512 toroidal grid, rendered through a precomputed LUT from deep indigo to electric cyan at 60 fps.

title: Distance Bloom — 2D SDF Morphing
tagline: Three primitives drift on Lissajous orbits; their blended signed-distance field is sliced into neon isocontour bands — cyan, magenta, amber, green — glowing brightest at each level-set boundary, animated in a WebGL fragment shader at 60 fps
technique: WebGL / 2D SDF / smooth union + subtraction / isocontour bands / GLSL fragment shader
description: Three primitives drift on Lissajous orbits with irrational frequencies, their blended signed-distance field sliced into neon isocontour bands — cyan, magenta, amber, green — glowing brightest at each level-set boundary, animated in a WebGL fragment shader at 60 fps.

title: Voronoi Stipple — Weighted Dot Etching
tagline: 600 seeds relax via Lloyd’s algorithm toward a density defined by a sum of 2-D Gaussians — thick charcoal dots in dark regions, hairline dots in light — producing a pen-plotter etching aesthetic on off-white paper; animated at 30 fps for 45 iterations then held
technique: canvas 2D / Voronoi Lloyd relaxation / weighted centroid stipple / spatial grid / density Gaussians
description: 600 seeds relax via Lloyd's algorithm toward a density-weighted configuration defined by a sum of 2-D Gaussians, producing thick charcoal dots in dense regions and hairline dots in sparse periphery for a pen-plotter etching aesthetic on off-white paper.

title: Halftone Tides — Interference Wave Dot Grid
tagline: A 40×40 halftone dot grid whose radii are driven by the superposition of four traveling plane waves — independent directions, spatial frequencies, and temporal frequencies — generating a traveling moiré-like interference pattern; deep indigo background with warm gold dots, animated at 60 fps
technique: canvas 2D / halftone dot grid / multi-wave interference / traveling sine waves / radius clamping
description: A 40×40 halftone dot grid whose radii are driven by the superposition of four traveling plane waves with independent directions, spatial frequencies, and temporal frequencies, generating a traveling moiré-like interference pattern in deep indigo with warm gold dots at 60 fps.

title: Quasicrystal — Five-Wave Interference
tagline: Five cosine plane waves at 72° intervals superimpose into a 5-fold quasicrystal field rendered pixel-by-pixel via ImageData; the pattern slowly rotates at 0.15 rad/s, cycling bright-cyan constructive peaks through a dark navy background in a seamless 60 fps loop
technique: canvas 2D / ImageData / quasicrystal / 5-wave plane-wave interference / cosine LUT / pixel shader
description: Five cosine plane waves at 72° intervals superimpose into a 5-fold quasicrystal field rendered pixel-by-pixel via ImageData, slowly rotating at 0.15 rad/s with a precomputed cosine LUT, cycling bright-cyan constructive peaks through dark navy in a seamless 60 fps loop.

title: Chladni Figures — Nodal Lines of a Vibrating Plate
tagline: Ten vibrational modes sin(m·πx)·sin(n·πy) of a square plate cycle with 1.5-second cross-fades; nodal lines where the field ≈ 0 glow sand-gold with white highlights against a near-black background, rendered pixel-by-pixel via ImageData at 60 fps
technique: canvas 2D / ImageData / Chladni figures / standing wave modes / colour LUT / pixel shader
description: Ten vibrational modes sin(m·πx)·sin(n·πy) of a square plate cycle with 1.5-second cross-fades, with nodal lines where the field approaches zero glowing sand-gold with white highlights against near-black, rendered pixel-by-pixel via ImageData at 60 fps.

title: The Gyroid Unfolds — Triply Periodic Minimal Surface
tagline: A WebGL fragment shader takes animated cross-sections of the gyroid implicit surface f = sin(x)cos(y)+sin(y)cos(z)+sin(z)cos(x); the z-slice sweeps −π to π every 20 seconds while the plane rocks ±10°, tracing labyrinthine gold level-set curves on a near-black field as topology evolves
technique: WebGL / GLSL fragment shader / implicit surface / level-set cross-section / triply periodic minimal surface
description: A WebGL fragment shader takes animated cross-sections of the gyroid implicit surface f = sin(x)cos(y)+sin(y)cos(z)+sin(z)cos(x), with the z-slice sweeping −π to π every 20 seconds while the plane rocks ±10°, tracing labyrinthine gold level-set curves on near-black as topology evolves.

title: L-System Branching Trees — Pen-Plotter Grammar
tagline: Two L-system grammars — a symmetric branching plant (F→F[+F]F[-F]F) and a baroque bush (F→FF-[-F+F+F]+[+F-F-F]) — grow stroke by stroke from axiom to fractal canopy; ink-black on warm cream, then copper on near-black
technique: canvas / L-system / turtle graphics / parametric grammar / requestAnimationFrame
description: Two L-system grammars — a symmetric branching plant (F→F[+F]F[-F]F) and a baroque bush (F→FF-[-F+F+F]+[+F-F-F]) — grow stroke by stroke from axiom to fractal canopy with line width tapering by depth, ink-black on cream and copper on near-black, in alternating seamless cycles.

title: Wave Harmonics Synthesizer
tagline: Build any waveform from sine waves — 8 harmonics with amplitude and phase sliders assemble square, sawtooth, and triangle waves live; watch Gibbs ringing appear harmonic by harmonic
technique: canvas / Fourier synthesis / harmonic series / waveform construction / interactive sliders / Gibbs phenomenon / requestAnimationFrame
description: An interactive Fourier synthesis workbench where eight sine-wave harmonics are displayed individually in the upper panel (each in a distinct hue) and their weighted sum appears live in the lower panel as a bright white waveform; amplitude and phase sliders let you construct square, sawtooth, and triangle waves from scratch, and the Convergence animation adds one harmonic per second so you can watch the Gibbs overshoot sharpen near every jump discontinuity.

title: Lorenz Attractor — Chaos Butterfly
tagline: Two trajectories a hair's breadth apart trace the double-wing strange attractor in real time — tune σ, ρ, β with sliders and watch the butterfly effect unfold in amber and teal
technique: canvas / Lorenz ODE / RK4 / chaos theory / interactive sliders / twin trajectory / alpha fade trail
description: Two Lorenz trajectories starting ε = 10⁻⁴ apart are integrated via fourth-order Runge-Kutta at dt = 0.005 and projected from 3D to 2D with a fixed rotation matrix; the amber path and teal twin diverge exponentially on the strange attractor, their luminous ghost trails fading each frame via canvas alpha-blend. Three sliders control σ (Prandtl), ρ (Rayleigh), and β live — any change restarts both trajectories so the butterfly effect is visible from the first step.

title: Double Pendulum — Chaos Made Visible
tagline: Two pendulums started 0.001° apart diverge into chaos — gold and teal trails encode exponential separation via the Lyapunov exponent, driven by exact Lagrangian RK4 equations
technique: canvas / Lagrangian double pendulum / chaos / twin trajectory / RK4 / interactive sliders
description: A double pendulum with two rigid arms is integrated via 4th-order Runge-Kutta using the exact Lagrangian equations of motion — no small-angle approximation. A twin pendulum started 0.001° offset in θ₁ diverges exponentially, with gold and teal trails whose separation embodies the Lyapunov exponent λ ≈ 3–7 s⁻¹. Initial angle sliders, mass-ratio control, and trail-length settings let the user explore the boundary between regular and chaotic motion. Trail color encodes time: older segments fade, recent segments glow bright.

title: Four Rules, Infinite Complexity: Game of Life
tagline: Four birth-and-death rules on a 120×80 toroidal grid: paint cells, load presets (Glider, Blinker, Pulsar, Gosper Gun), watch the pattern library update live, and toggle heat-map age tinting
technique: canvas / Conway's Game of Life / cellular automaton / ImageData / pattern detection
description: Conway's Game of Life on a 120×80 toroidal grid rendered via ImageData for maximum speed. Four preset patterns (Glider, Blinker, Pulsar, Gosper Glider Gun) are loadable with one click; cells can be painted by mouse-drag. A sidebar keeps the four rules always visible alongside a live pattern-library scan detecting Blocks, Beehives, Blinkers, Toads, Beacons, and Gliders in real time. An optional heat-map mode tints long-lived cells deep red and newborn cells bright amber, making the temporal structure of the automaton visible. Speed ranges from 1 to 60 generations per second.

title: Fourier Series Builder — Harmonics Summoned One by One
tagline: Toggle H1–H10 harmonics and watch square, sawtooth, and triangle waves emerge from chained rotating phasors — the left panel spins the arrows, the right panel draws the composite wave in real time
technique: canvas / Fourier series / phasor visualization / epicycles / harmonic synthesis / requestAnimationFrame
description: An interactive Fourier series builder split across two panels: on the left, each active harmonic is drawn as a rotating phasor arrow chained tip-to-tail at frequencies 1x through 10x; on the right, the tip position is recorded continuously to draw the composite waveform. Preset buttons load the classic square, sawtooth, and triangle wave harmonic recipes; the H1–H10 toggle buttons let you add or remove individual harmonics to hear — and see — exactly what each one contributes. A slide-out panel explains Fourier’s theorem in plain prose with inline formulas for all three waveforms.

title: Double-Slit Diffraction — Light as a Wave
tagline: Animate wavefronts from 1, 2, or 5 slits; watch the Fraunhofer interference pattern update live as you tune slit separation, width, and wavelength across the visible spectrum
technique: canvas / wave interference / double-slit diffraction / Huygens principle / ImageData / requestAnimationFrame
description: A split-canvas simulation of Fraunhofer diffraction. The left 60% renders a top-down wave-propagation view: a plane wave hits a barrier with N slit openings; cylindrical waves radiate outward from each slit and their superposition creates a live animated interference pattern. The right 40% is the detector screen, computing the analytical intensity I(θ) = sinc²(πa·sinθ/λ) × [sin(Nδ)/(N·sinδ)]² for the current controls and displaying it as a bright vertical intensity bar with a profile curve. Sliders control slit separation d (0.5λ – 5λ), slit width a (0.1λ – 2λ), and wavelength (380 – 700 nm with live color preview); buttons toggle between single slit, double slit, and 5-slit diffraction grating. A slide-out panel covers the Huygens-Fresnel principle, fringe position equations, Young’s 1801 experiment, and the quantum-mechanical connection via the de Broglie wavelength.

title: SIR Epidemic: The Curve We Tried to Flatten
tagline: 300 particles bounce and infect in real time as the live S/I/R chart traces the bell-shaped epidemic curve — tune β, γ, and vaccination to flatten it
technique: canvas / SIR epidemic model / particle simulation / requestAnimationFrame
description: A dual-panel epidemic simulation: on the left 300 colour-coded particles (blue=susceptible, red=infected, green=recovered) bounce around a box, infecting neighbours on contact; on the right a live S/I/R line chart records the population counts over time, revealing the characteristic bell-shaped infected curve. Four sliders tune transmission rate β (0.1–1.0), recovery rate γ (0.05–0.5), initial infected count, and vaccination fraction; the reproduction number R₀ = β/γ updates in real time with a colour cue (green when R₀ < 1, red otherwise). A slide-out panel explains the dS/dt, dI/dt, dR/dt differential equations and the flatten-the-curve insight in plain prose.

title: Ising Model — The Temperature of Order
tagline: A 200×200 spin lattice driven by Metropolis–Hastings Monte Carlo — drag temperature through the Curie point and watch ferromagnetic domains melt into paramagnetic chaos
technique: canvas / Ising model / Metropolis–Hastings Monte Carlo / ImageData / statistical mechanics
description: A 200×200 spin lattice of +1/−1 magnetic dipoles (warm linen and deep indigo) evolves under the Metropolis–Hastings Monte Carlo algorithm with N² flip attempts per frame. The temperature slider spans T = 0.5 (ordered ferromagnet) through Tc ≈ 2.269 (Onsager's exact 2D critical point, marked) to T = 4.0 (paramagnetic disorder). Only two Boltzmann lookup values are needed per frame (exp(−4/T) and exp(−8/T)). A live readout shows current temperature, net magnetisation M, and a rolling sparkline of |M| over time so the phase transition is graphable. Clicking any cell flips that spin as a defect injection. An info pane covers spins as magnetic dipoles, the Boltzmann factor, the Curie temperature, domain formation, and Onsager's 1944 exact solution.

title: Mandelbrot + Julia — Interactive Explorer
tagline: Hover over the Mandelbrot set to see the corresponding Julia set appear instantly — orbit traces show why each point escapes or stays
technique: WebGL / Mandelbrot set / Julia set / complex iteration / GLSL fragment shader / escape-time coloring
description: A dual-panel WebGL fractal explorer that makes the Mandelbrot-to-Julia connection interactive. The left two-thirds renders the Mandelbrot set via a GLSL fragment shader with smooth escape-time colouring (continuous iteration count via n − log₂(log₂(|z|)), no banding). Hovering any point c in the Mandelbrot canvas immediately renders the corresponding Julia set J_c in a right panel using the same shader with c as a uniform. An orbit-trace overlay (2D canvas, pointer-events none) draws the iteration path for the hovered c as step-coloured circles connected by lines, showing whether the orbit escapes or converges. Controls: click-drag to pan, scroll-wheel to zoom toward the cursor, double-click to re-centre, max-iterations slider (64–2048), and a reset button. The GalleryPanel educational drawer covers what the Mandelbrot set is, how smooth colouring works, the M to Julia correspondence, and the infinite complexity of the boundary.

title: Ulam Spiral — The Hidden Order of Primes
tagline: 200 000 integers arranged in a square spiral — primes glow in amber, revealing mysterious diagonal streaks that no one has fully explained
technique: canvas / Ulam spiral / sieve of Eratosthenes / ImageData / zoom-pan / number theory
description: An interactive canvas that renders the Ulam prime spiral for all integers 1 to 200 000. A Sieve of Eratosthenes pre-computes smallest prime factors in O(N log log N); the spiral coordinates are stored in a 500×500 Uint32Array for O(1) cell lookup. Three colour modes expose different mathematical structure: monochrome (prime amber vs composite near-black) makes the diagonal streaks visible at a glance; residue mod 4 (teal for p≡1, rose for p≡3, Dirichlet's theorem) shows the prime race between the two residue classes; smallest-prime-factor colouring reveals the sieve of Eratosthenes itself laid over the grid. Zoom via scroll wheel (cursor stays fixed), pan via click-drag, and pinch-to-zoom on touch. Hovering any cell shows the integer and its full prime factorisation. A slide-out educational drawer explains the spiral layout, why diagonals form via prime-dense quadratic polynomials (Euler's lucky polynomial n²+n+41), Dirichlet's theorem on primes in arithmetic progressions, and the open questions that remain unsolved.

title: The Butterfly Effect — Lorenz Attractor in 3D
tagline: 50 000-point Lorenz orbit glows deep-violet to cyan in WebGL; press Nudge to spawn a coral twin 0.001 units away and watch chaos theory happen in real time
technique: chaos / Lorenz attractor / WebGL / RK4 integration / line-strip / additive blending
description: A WebGL 2 line-strip renderer integrates the Lorenz strange attractor via fourth-order Runge-Kutta at dt=0.005, maintaining a 50 001-point ring buffer coloured deep-violet (oldest) to electric-cyan (newest) through additive blending. The camera auto-rotates around the Z axis while mouse-drag controls full yaw and pitch. A Nudge button spawns a second trajectory starting 0.001 units away in warm coral, demonstrating sensitive dependence on initial conditions as the two paths diverge exponentially. Three sliders tune σ (Prandtl, 1–20), ρ (Rayleigh, 1–50), and β (geometric, 0.1–8) live, reshaping the attractor instantly. A slide-out panel covers Lorenz's 1963 atmospheric-convection model, the physical meaning of each variable, what chaos means mathematically (Lyapunov exponent λ₁ ≈ 0.9), and the butterfly-effect origin story, with an inline SVG diagram of the two-lobed wing structure.

title: Charge Canvas — Electric Fields and Equipotentials
tagline: Drag point charges around a canvas and watch Coulomb field lines (gold) and equipotential contours (teal) reform live — a hands-on superposition simulator
technique: canvas / Coulomb's law / electric field lines / equipotential contours / superposition / interactive drag / marching squares
description: An interactive 2D canvas that simulates the electric field and equipotential landscape of up to eight user-placed point charges. Field lines (warm gold) are traced by Euler streamline integration from each positive charge — stepping 2 px along the normalised E vector until a negative charge is reached or the canvas edge is hit. Equipotential contours (cool teal dashes) are computed by evaluating V = Σ kq/r on an 80×80 grid and contouring at ten evenly-spaced iso-levels via marching squares with saddle-point disambiguation. Dragging any charge re-draws everything within one animation frame, capped at 60 fps via a dirty-flag pattern. Clicking empty canvas adds a charge (sign alternates +/−); clicking an existing charge removes it. A slide-out panel explains Coulomb's law, the superposition principle, what field-line density encodes, and why equipotentials are always perpendicular to field lines.

title: Fourier Epicycles — Draw Anything with Rotating Circles
tagline: Sketch any closed shape and watch an army of orbiting circles reconstruct it — a live Discrete Fourier Transform visualiser in gold and navy
technique: canvas / Discrete Fourier Transform / epicycles / complex phasors / arc-length resampling / interactive drawing / harmonic decomposition
description: An interactive canvas that decomposes any closed 2D path into rotating circles (epicycles) via the Discrete Fourier Transform. Both x and y coordinates are packed into a single complex signal z[n] = x[n] + i·y[n]; the DFT X[k] = (1/N) Σ z[n]·e^(−i2πkn/N) extracts N frequency components, each with a radius |X[k]| and starting angle arg(X[k]). Components are sorted by amplitude so the +/− buttons add harmonics in order of visual impact. Default paths are a parametric heart (16 sin³t, −(13 cos t − 5 cos 2t − 2 cos 3t − cos 4t)) and a lemniscate of Bernoulli. Clicking ✏ Draw Path enters draw mode: drag any closed shape, release, and the piece resamples the stroke to 512 arc-length-uniform points, runs the O(N²) DFT (< 30 ms), and immediately starts the epicycle replay. The white trace fades in 16 alpha buckets; after one 5-second revolution it perfectly retraces the approximation. A slide-out educational panel covers the DFT formula, what each circle's radius and speed represent, how JPEG compression uses the same idea, and the Gibbs phenomenon at sharp corners.

title: Rössler Attractor — Ensemble Divergence
tagline: Seven Rössler orbits started 0.01 apart spiral and diverge exponentially — deterministic chaos on a strange attractor with Möbius-band topology
technique: canvas / Rössler attractor / chaos theory / RK4 integration / multi-trajectory / Lyapunov exponent / x-y projection
description: Seven Rössler systems (dx/dt = -(y+z), dy/dt = x+a·y, dz/dt = b+z·(x-c)) start with x₀ offsets of 0.01 between neighbors, integrated simultaneously via 4th-order Runge-Kutta and projected onto the x-y plane. Each orbit traces a colored trail (spectral palette: red through violet) that persists and fades over 500 frames on a near-black canvas, showing the characteristic spiral-and-fold topology of the Rössler strange attractor. A live Chaos Meter tracks max Euclidean distance in x-y from the center trajectory, revealing how the Lyapunov exponent (λ ≈ 0.07 s⁻¹) slowly amplifies the 0.01-unit offset. Sliders control parameters a, b, c, and initial x₀; dragging c below 5 collapses all seven trails to a single periodic orbit.
